meetingType: Board of Zoning Appeal Virtual Public Meeting

items.title: 41 Sherman Street: special permit for rear addition to non-conforming single-family home
items.summary: Owners of 41 Sherman Street are seeking a special permit from the Board of Zoning Appeal to build a rear addition on a pre-existing non-conforming single-family home, with the addition encroaching into the side-yard setbacks. The property sits in a Residence C-1 zone. A virtual public hearing is scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 6:00 P.M.
items.body:
6:00 P.M. CASE NO. BZA-1203860
41 SHERMAN STREET — Residence C-1 Zone
KEERIL MAKAN & JOANNA RAKOFF – C/O SARA YOFFE
Special Permit: Pre-existing non-conforming single-family home proposing rear addition within side yard setbacks. Art. 5.000, Sec. 5.31 (Table of Dimensional Requirements). Art. 8.000, Sec. 8.22.2.d (Non-Conforming Structure). Art. 10.000, Sec. 10.40 (Special Permit).

history.action: Virtual Public Hearing scheduled at 6:00 P.M. before the Board of Zoning Appeal

items.title: 80 Gerry's Landing Road: special permit for BB&N School educational-use addition
items.summary: Buckingham Browne & Nichols School is seeking a special permit from the Board of Zoning Appeal to build an addition to its existing building at 80 Gerry's Landing Road for continued educational use. The property is in a Residence C-1 zone. A virtual public hearing is scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 6:15 P.M.
items.body:
6:15 P.M. CASE NO. BZA-1205111
80 GERRY'S LANDING ROAD — Residence C-1 Zone
BUCKINGHAM BROWNE & NICHOLS SCHOOL – C/O SISIA DAGLIAN, ARCHITECT
Special Permit: To build an addition to existing BB&N building for continued educational use. Art. 4.000, Sec. 4.50 & Sec. 4.56.c (Educational Use). Art. 10.000, Sec. 10.40 (Special Permit).

items.title: 723-731 Cambridge Street: variance to add three stories atop mixed-use building
items.summary: The owner of 723-731 Cambridge Street is seeking a variance from the Board of Zoning Appeal to add three stories on top of a pre-existing three-story mixed-use building (ground-floor retail with residential units above). A small portion of the addition would sit within the required setback for a primary and secondary street, and the petitioner also seeks relief from facade transparency rules along Marion Street. The property is in the Cambridge Street District-6 zone. A virtual public hearing is scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 6:30 P.M.
items.body:
6:30 P.M. CASE NO. BZA-1204609
723-731 CAMBRIDGE STREET — Cambridge Street District -6
723-731 CAMBRIDGE STREET LLC, MANUEL BARROS – C/O JAMES J. RAFFERTY, ESQ.
Variance: To construct three additional stories atop pre-existing three story mixed-use structure containing ground floor retail with residential dwelling units above. A small portion of the addition will be located within the required setback for a primary and secondary street. Petitioner also seeks relief from the facade transparency regulations for the proposed facade on Marion Street. Art. 8.000, Sec. 8.22.3 (Non-Conforming Structure). Art. 17.000, Sec. 17.705.3 (Table of Site Plan Standards). Sec. 17.705.4.4 (Façade Transparency). Art. 10.000, Sec. 10.30 (Variance).

history.action: Virtual Public Hearing scheduled at 6:30 P.M. before the Board of Zoning Appeal
